description_long: This church features a tall, pointed spire rising from a square bell tower, which is adorned with clock faces on each side and arched windows. The main body of the church is rectangular with a steeply pitched, dark roof and large, vertical windows along the nave. The facade of the bell tower is detailed with stonework and decorative elements, while the attached nave and side structures are simpler, with smooth, light-colored walls. The entrance appears to be at the base of the bell tower, which is the most ornate and prominent part of the building. The church's architectural style is reminiscent of Gothic Revival, with its pointed arches, tall spire, and vertical emphasis. The structure is substantial, likely constructed from stone and concrete, and is designed to accommodate congregational gatherings.
description: A large stone church with a tall, pointed spire atop a clock-adorned bell tower and a long, steeply roofed nave with vertical windows. The facade is ornate, while the main body is simpler and light-colored.
description_view_0: The front view highlights the tall bell tower with its pointed spire, clock faces, and the main entrance at the base, as well as the long nave extending to the right.
description_view_1: This angled view emphasizes the steep, dark roof of the nave and the verticality of the spire, with the side walls and windows visible.
description_view_2: The side view showcases the full length of the nave, the repetitive vertical windows, and the attached side structure, with the bell tower at the end.
description_view_3: This rear-angled view focuses on the back of the church and the base of the bell tower, with less detail visible on the facade.
